_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: TURP-Transurethral Resection of the Prostate, BPH (Monopolar)
source_files: Prostatetctomy,TURP of the Prostate for BPH_Medco.pdf
Prostatetctomy,TURP of the Prostate for BPH_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Acute urinary retention, Failed voiding trials
Prostate abscess, difficulty with clean intermittent catheterization and obstructive azoospermia.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment
USG with prostate size and Post Void Volume +/- PSA, +/- uroflowmetry report confirming the diagnosis

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the USG with prostate size and Post Void Volume +/- PSA, +/- uroflowmetry report confirming the diagnosis and need for the surgery submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed indoor case papers with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Was Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes submitted?
Was the Detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes or USG with prostate size, Post Void Volume, PSA, or uroflowmetry report are not indicative of the proposed procedure.
Clinical notes indicate fewer than 2 episodes of urinary retention for TURP procedure without clear justification.
USG report confirming diagnosis and need for surgery not submitted at pre-authorization.
Histopathology report for the resected tissue is missing at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Transurethral resection of the prostate or TURP prostate is resected from an endoscopic approach, TURP also be used to unroof prostatic abscesses.
surgical_options: Conventional monopolar TURP (M-TURP) is in practice. With the use of a bipolar generator, both the active and return electrodes are contained within the instrument.
